display.description: Free weather API: geocoding, hourly/daily forecasts, and current conditions worldwide. No credential required — this is the reference no-auth connection.
display.setup_help: **No credential is needed.** Open-Meteo is free for non-commercial use without an API key or account — this connection works out of the box; just ensure `api.open-meteo.com` and `geocoding-api.open-meteo.com` are in the http server's allowed hosts. For commercial use, an API-key plan exists: see <https://open-meteo.com/en/pricing> (a commercial key changes the hostname to `customer-api.open-meteo.com`, which would be a separate connection).

operations.summary: Resolve a place name to latitude/longitude coordinates
operations.description: Searches Open-Meteo's geocoding database for places matching a name and returns candidates with latitude, longitude, country, admin region, timezone, and population. Use this FIRST whenever the user names a place rather than giving coordinates — forecast and current-weather both require lat/lon. Read-only, no side effects. NOTE: this operation lives on a different host than base_url; its path is an absolute URL (geocoding-api.open-meteo.com, allowlisted above).

name.description: Place name to search for, e.g. "Birmingham". Fuzzy matching applies for 3+ characters; 1-2 characters require an exact match.

count.description: Maximum number of candidate places to return.

language.description: Lowercase ISO-639-1 language code for localized place names, e.g. "en", "de".

countryCode.description: Optional uppercase ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 filter, e.g. "US" — restricts results to one country. Useful to disambiguate names that exist in many countries.

operations.summary: Get hourly and/or daily weather forecast for coordinates
operations.description: Returns a multi-day forecast for a lat/lon point: hourly time-series and/or daily aggregates, each variable selected by name. Use this for anything about the future or about time-series detail (rain tomorrow, temperatures this week). For a one-shot "what is it like right now", prefer current-weather — smaller response, no variable-list thinking. Read-only, no side effects. Returns parallel arrays under hourly.time / hourly.<variable> and daily.time / daily.<variable>, plus per-variable units.

latitude.description: WGS84 latitude in decimal degrees, e.g. 33.52 for Birmingham, AL.

longitude.description: WGS84 longitude in decimal degrees, e.g. -86.81 for Birmingham, AL.

hourly.description: Comma-separated hourly variable names, e.g. "temperature_2m", "temperature_2m,precipitation,wind_speed_10m". Common values: temperature_2m, relative_humidity_2m, precipitation, precipitation_probability, wind_speed_10m, cloud_cover, weather_code. Omit to skip hourly data.

daily.description: Comma-separated daily aggregate names, e.g. "temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_sum". Common values: temperature_2m_max, temperature_2m_min, precipitation_sum, precipitation_probability_max, sunrise, sunset, weather_code, wind_speed_10m_max. Requires timezone to be set. Omit to skip daily data.

forecast_days.description: Number of forecast days to return. Keep small (1-3) to keep responses compact.

timezone.description: IANA timezone for local-time timestamps, e.g. "America/Chicago", or "auto" to resolve from the coordinates. Default is GMT. Required when requesting daily aggregates.

operations.summary: Get current conditions (temperature, wind) for coordinates
operations.description: Convenience wrapper over the same /v1/forecast endpoint that returns ONLY a compact `current` block for a lat/lon point — one timestamped reading per requested variable, no time-series arrays. Prefer this over forecast when the question is about right now; use forecast when the user asks about later today, tomorrow, or trends. Read-only, no side effects.

latitude.description: WGS84 latitude in decimal degrees, e.g. 33.52 for Birmingham, AL.

longitude.description: WGS84 longitude in decimal degrees, e.g. -86.81 for Birmingham, AL.

current.description: Comma-separated current-condition variable names. The default covers temperature and wind; other common values: relative_humidity_2m, apparent_temperature, precipitation, weather_code, cloud_cover.

timezone.description: IANA timezone for the reading's local timestamp, e.g. "America/Chicago", or "auto". Default is GMT.
